## FHIR Queries for Psoriasis Patient Identification

### Query 1 — Condition (Psoriasis, SNOMED CT)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|9014002&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 2 — MedicationRequest (Methotrexate, RxNorm)
```
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|105585&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

## Design Notes

| Dimension | Query 1 — Condition | Query 2 — MedicationRequest |
|---|---|---|
| **Code** | SNOMED `9014002` (Psoriasis) | RxNorm `105585` (methotrexate ingredient-level) |
| **Recall** | High for explicitly coded psoriasis | Over-recalls: RA, malignancy, ectopic pregnancy also use MTX |
| **Precision** | May miss if coded as subtype only (see note below) | Low — requires downstream filtering |
| **`_include`** | Hydrates `Patient` resources in same bundle | Same — avoids a second round-trip per patient |

### Subtype Coverage (SNOMED Hierarchy)
If your server supports hierarchical code expansion (i.e., `:below` modifier), prefer:
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|9014002&_include=Condition:subject
```
This captures subtypes such as plaque psoriasis (`200965001`), pustular psoriasis (`9014002` descendants), psoriatic erythroderma, etc.

---

## Union Logic (Post-Query)

```
psoriasis_patients =
  { r.subject.reference for r in Query1.entry } 
  ∪ 
  { r.subject.reference for r in Query2.entry }
```

**Important:** Because Query 2 over-recalls, treat the union result as a **candidate cohort** requiring a secondary filter — e.g., confirm MTX patients also carry a psoriasis/psoriatic arthritis `Condition` record, or review `MedicationRequest.reasonCode` / `MedicationRequest.reasonReference` to exclude RA/malignancy indications before finalizing the psoriasis phenotype.

### 🔍 Query Rationale

| Query | Purpose |
|---|---|

| **`status=active`** | Filters to currently active prescriptions only |

| **`code:text=methotrexate`** | Fallback text search for systems without coded medications |

## FHIR Queries — Methotrexate MedicationRequests (RxNorm)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|105585&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|226449&status=active,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### RxNorm Code Rationale

| RxNorm Code | Concept | Level |
|---|---|---|
| **105585** | methotrexate | Ingredient (most broadly matched) |
| **226449** | methotrexate sodium | Ingredient (salt form, commonly stored separately) |

Both ingredient-level codes are queried because EHR systems vary in which they use as the canonical code. Product-level (branded/dose-form) codes inherit from these ingredients but may not chain reliably in all FHIR implementations.

---

### Parameter Decisions

| Parameter | Value | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `code` | RxNorm ingredient codes | Medication-level search across brands/generics |
| `status` | `active,completed` | Captures both ongoing and historically treated patients |
| `_include` | `MedicationRequest:patient` | Returns linked Patient resources in the same Bundle |

---

### ⚠️ Cross-Indication Contamination Warning

As noted, methotrexate is heavily cross-indicated. A medication-only cohort will include:

| Indication | Typical Dose | Route |
|---|---|---|
| Rheumatoid arthritis | 7.5–25 mg/week | PO / SC / IM |
| Psoriasis / PsA | 7.5–25 mg/week | PO / SC |
| Oncology (ALL, lymphoma, osteosarcoma) | High-dose (g/m²) | IV |
| Ectopic pregnancy | 50 mg/m² single dose | IM |

**Recommended disambiguation approach:** Chain these results against `Condition` resources to filter by relevant diagnosis codes (e.g., ICD-10 `L40.*` for psoriasis, `M07.*` for psoriatic arthritis) using a `_revinclude` or separate `Condition?subject=<id>&code=...` query per returned patient.
